title: The compilation pipeline and its stages
description: Source flows through lexing, parsing, semantic/type analysis, IR generation, optimization, and codegen, each refining a representation with its own contract.
body: Use when reasoning about where a compiler responsibility belongs or where a bug originates. The front end (lex, parse, type-check) turns text into a typed AST; the middle end lowers to an IR and optimizes; the back end generates target code. Each stage consumes one representation and produces a more refined one under a contract. Do not blur stage responsibilities or debug downstream without checking the upstream contract held. Actions: locate a symptom by identifying which stage's invariant is violated and inspect that stage's output. Validation signals include per-stage verifiers confirming each representation is well-formed. Risk boundary: misattributing a bug to the wrong stage wastes effort and can mask the real cause; verify the producing stage's output before editing the consumer.
